old_claim.note: Threshing & Winnowing provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The depicted process receives harvested barley sheaves and separates their grain, so cereal agriculture supplies the required material stream.

source_shape.source_locator: Bottom Register describes reaping barley, bundling and transporting sheaves, donkey trampling on the threshing floor, and winnowing.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The donkey-treading operation is one stage in a directly depicted agricultural barley-harvest sequence.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Cereal production supplies the crop being processed, while the separate Greek sledge node is an independent implementation rather than this scene's precursor.

ontology_before: One specific Neolithic Greek threshing-sledge instance was treated as the enabler of a later independent Egyptian treading method.
ontology_after: The Raemkai scene depends on the documented cereal-production system without claiming descent from another regional threshing implementation.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: agriculture replaces threshing_and_winnowing as the crop-system prerequisite for animal_powered_threshing.
would_reject_if: The Raemkai register were shown not to depict cultivated barley harvesting and processing or to document direct adoption of the Greek sledge tradition.
short_reason: Agriculture supplies the barley; a distinct Greek sledge is not a causal prerequisite.
